CHARTS / BASKETS
% RETURN, REBASED · EOD 2026-07-08
DEFENSIVES LEADCyclicals -3.1% vs defensives on the month
Rotation ReadCitrini · claude

The rotation is defensive and rotational, and you should treat the +6.91% one-month return in XLF and +6.74% in XLV as the market voting against the YTD leadership of XLK (+24.26%) and XLE (+21.51%). The stocks working right now are not the ones that led the first half. Semis posted -3.8% over the last month and AI infra -4.8%, while GLP-1 names ripped +17.8%, biotech +16.0%, and cyber +16.2%. That is a narrow, thematic bid into healthcare-adjacent and beaten-down growth, with the Mag 7 flat at -1.5% on the month and barely positive +0.8% YTD. You pair this with XLY at -0.04% one-month and -2.54% YTD, XLC at -0.86% and -5.79%, and the picture is clear: the consumer-discretionary and communication-services complexes are rolling under their own weight while defensives and financials absorb the flows. The breadth signal is real, but it is breadth into safety, not breadth into growth.

The central tension is whether XLF and XLV leading means a healthy broadening or an early risk-off disguise. XLI at +4.45% one-month and +14.79% YTD gives the cyclical case oxygen, and XLI/XLP sits at a z-score of 1.87 in the top 96% of its yearly range, confirming industrials still own cyclicals over staples. But utilities at +4.71% one-month and +5.53% YTD, alongside staples at +1.73% and real estate at +1.45%, tell you the defensive bid is broad and persistent. The EMB/TLT ratio at a z-score of 2.06 in the top 99% of its range means emerging-market debt is crushing duration, and SPY/TLT at 2.11 in the top 95% says stocks remain the preferred vehicle over bonds. That cross-asset signal supports the risk-on read. The conflict: cyclicals and defensives are both bid, and that rarely lasts. The resolution comes from XLY. If consumer discretionary continues to underperform at -2.54% YTD while defensives accelerate, this is a late-cycle rotation and you cut cyclical exposure into strength.

Defensives and cyclicals are both bid, but discretionary is breaking down: this is either a healthy broadening or a flight to safety disguised as rotation.

BULL

XLF at +6.91% one-month, XLI at +14.79% YTD, and XLI/XLP at a z-score of 1.87 in the top 96% of its range confirm cyclical relative strength is intact and the rotation into financials and industrials is real risk appetite finding new homes.

BEAR

XLV at +6.74%, XLU at +4.71%, and XLP at +8.78% YTD show defensives absorbing capital while XLY sits at -2.54% YTD and XLC at -5.79%, a signature of deteriorating consumer leadership that precedes broader weakness.

TELL

XLY/SPY at a z-score of -2.14 in the bottom 3% of its yearly range: if this ratio stabilizes and turns, the broadening is healthy; if it keeps making new lows, the defensive bid wins and you fade cyclicals.

⚠ XLI/XLP at a z-score of **1.87** in the top **96%** of its range says cyclicals dominate defensives and the risk-on rotation is healthy. vs XLV at **+6.74%** one-month, XLU at **+4.71%**, and XLP at **+8.78%** YTD show defensives receiving simultaneous capital inflows, a classic risk-off companion signal.: Both can be true early in a rotation when capital leaves tech and scatters, but if XLY at **-2.54%** YTD continues to deteriorate, the defensive bid is the real signal and cyclicals are a head-fade. Trust the discretionary breakdown: it leads the consumer, and the consumer leads the cycle.

The ratios flashing mean-reversion risk are XLC/SPY at a z-score of -2.38 in the bottom 5% of its yearly range with a YTD of -13.45%, and URA/SPY at -2.02 in the bottom 1% with -17.15% YTD. Both are compressed enough that a snapback squeeze is the higher-probability trade than continuation, and you cover shorts into weakness there.

The confirmed trend extremes are XBI/XLV at 2.23 in the top 100% of its range with +28.23% YTD and IBB/XLV at 1.99 also in the top 100% with +11.17% YTD, backed by biotech theme returns of +16.0% one-month: you do not fade those, you add on pullbacks. SPY/TLT at 2.11 in the top 95% and QQQ/TLT at 1.94 in the top 90% are stretched but confirmed by the equity-leadership rotation away from tech and into healthcare and financials, so the cross-asset risk-on signal holds until TTL/SPY at -2.02 in the bottom 5% stops making new lows.

MCHI/SPY at -2.16 in the bottom 5% with -22.14% YTD is a value trap until it base-builds. SOXX/SMH at 1.99 in the top 91% with +12.99% YTD is a quiet but important signal: the equal-weight semis are outperforming the cap-weighted, confirming the narrow AI-leveraged names are rolling while the broader chip complex holds up.

That supports the broadening thesis at the index level even as theme-level AI infra at -4.8% and semis at -3.8% lag.
